questions.prompt: Minimum viable CI for a typical web app PR?

choices.text: Test + lint + type-check + build, in parallel jobs. Each catches different bug categories: tests catch behavior regressions, lint catches style and common pitfalls, type-check catches type errors that runtime tests might miss, build catches integration/dependency issues that show up only in production. Running in parallel keeps total wall time low. Pair with branch protection requiring all four to pass before merge — broken code can't ship. Add caching via setup-node's `cache: 'npm'` to keep builds fast.

questions.explanation: Test + lint + type-check + build in parallel. Catches different bug categories.

questions.prompt: Concurrent pushes to a PR trigger multiple CI runs; only the latest matters. How to stop wasted runs?

choices.text: Add concurrency control to the workflow: `concurrency: { group: 'ci-${{ github.ref }}', cancel-in-progress: true }`. New runs in the same group (same PR) cancel previous in-progress runs automatically. Saves CI minutes and reflects current state (you don't care about results from outdated commits). For main pushes, usually disable cancellation — each commit's CI should complete for audit trail. Concurrency control is one of the highest-ROI CI tunings; standard pattern for active PR-heavy teams.

questions.explanation: Concurrency control with cancel-in-progress. Standard for active teams.
